channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: South Africa’s Trade Partnerships with the Middle East Face Geopolitical Challenges
item.description: South Africa has been actively working to diversify its trade and investment partners, increasingly looking towards Middle Eastern economies. These partnerships aim to help the country move up the min...

item.title: Exploring the Benefits and Drawbacks of Salary Sacrifice to Reduce Tax Bands
item.description: Sky News' Money newsletter, which reaches over 170,000 subscribers, provides weekly insights and guidance on personal finance topics. This upcoming Friday, the newsletter will examine the advantages a...

item.title: History of Carter Bridge: Lagos' 125-Year-Old Colonial Link Set for Demolition and Reconstruction
item.description: Carter Bridge, one of Lagos' oldest structures, was first built in 1901 during British colonial rule under Governor Sir Gilbert Thomas Carter, after whom it was named. Back then, Lagos Island was the ...

item.title: Tanzania fights new drug threat in food products
item.description: Tanzania is facing a serious challenge in its fight against drugs. Criminals are becoming more dangerous, now even putting drugs in biscuits and other foods, aiming to secretly trap young people and u...

item.title: Janelle Dodd tackles period poverty through community-led initiatives
item.description: Janelle Dodd, a crafter from Sydney, has spent nearly a decade leading the Ryde team of Days for Girls, an international not-for-profit focused on addressing period poverty. The organisation creates m...

item.title: Brumbies face scheduling challenges due to Wallabies player rest protocols
item.description: The ACT Brumbies are navigating a tricky start to their Super Rugby Pacific season, compounded by Rugby Australia's player management protocols. Head coach Stephen Larkham faces a scheduling challenge...

item.title: Nigeria Withdraws Ex-EFCC Chairman from Diezani Trial Over Credibility Concerns
item.description: For UK court matter wey concern former Nigerian Petroleum Minister, Diezani Alison-Madueke, authorities don withdraw Abdulrasheed Bawa, ex-chairman of EFCC, from di witness list. Bawa suppose testify ...

item.title: Matildas draw 3-3 with South Korea, finish second in Group A at Women's Asian Cup 2026
item.description: The Matildas played out an entertaining but costly 3-3 draw against South Korea in their final Group A match of the AFC Women's Asian Cup 2026 at Stadium Australia, Sydney, in front of 60,279 spectato...

item.title: Johana Ng'eno: MP aliyechagua maisha rahisi bila ulinzi wa karani
item.description: Johana Ng'eno, MP wa Emurua Dikirr, alikuwa mtu wa kipekee ambaye aliamua kuacha bodyguards zake, marafiki wa karibu, na wasaidizi ili kuishi maisha rahisi. Kinyume na wengine wa wabunge wanaotembea n...

item.title: Father fatally shoots son during family argument in Koronadal
item.description: In Koronadal City, a tragic incident occurred on Saturday, March 7, when a 53-year-old father, reportedly intoxicated, fatally shot his 32-year-old son with a 12-gauge shotgun in their yard in Baranga...

item.title: Minor Explosion at U.S. Embassy in Oslo Causes No Injuries, Norwegian Police Investigate
item.description: Early on Sunday, March 8, 2026, a loud explosion occurred at the U.S. Embassy compound in western Oslo, Norway, around 1:00 a.m. local time (0000 GMT). Norwegian police confirmed that the blast struck...

item.title: Swinney may block US military flights at Prestwick Airport pending government clarification
item.description: Scottish First Minister John Swinney has stated that he would consider banning US military aircraft from using the state-owned Prestwick Airport if it is confirmed that they are involved in military s...

item.title: Schedule and Viewing Information for India vs New Zealand T20 World Cup 2026 Final
item.description: The ICC Men’s T20 World Cup 2026 is set to conclude with an exciting final between India and New Zealand at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad on Sunday, March 8, starting at 7:00 pm IST. Cricket ...

item.title: Government Prepares for Fuel Supply Amid Rising Global Oil Prices
item.description: Pakistan is set to receive three petrol shipments by tomorrow, Petroleum Minister Ali Pervaiz Malik informed during a briefing on Sunday. This comes as global oil prices rise due to the ongoing confli...

item.title: Rising Food Insecurity in West and Central Africa: Causes and Needed Actions
item.description: Countries in West and Central Africa are facing a worsening food crisis affecting tens of millions of people. By late 2025, around 41.8 million were already experiencing severe food insecurity, and pr...

item.title: Authorities investigate house fire in Saint-Tharcisius where human remains were found
item.description: Late Friday night, emergency crews responded to a house fire at approximately 3:30 a.m. in Saint-Tharcisius, located in Quebec’s Bas-Saint-Laurent region. The fire occurred at a single-family home on ...

item.title: Comedian I Go Save Criticizes Tonto Dikeh Over Deliverance Session With School Pupil
item.description: Nigerian comedian I Go Save don yan about one video wey dey circulate online wey show Nollywood actress Tonto Dikeh dey perform deliverance for one school pupil during her outreach programme. Tonto Di...

item.title: President Marcos Urges LGUs to Digitize Aid Distribution for Seniors and Enforce Anti-Epal Policy
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. called on local government units (LGUs) to speed up and simplify the distribution of financial assistance to senior citizens by adopting digital methods. In his recent v...

item.title: DBSA Proposes Feasibility Support for Key National Infrastructure Projects
item.description: The Development Bank of Southern Africa (DBSA) is stepping up its role in supporting the government’s infrastructure initiatives by offering to conduct feasibility assessments and manage project manag...

item.title: Kenya Prisons Officers Celebrate New Mothers at Homa Bay Hospital on International Women’s Day
item.description: At Homa Bay Teaching and Referral Hospital, the maternity ward was buzzing with smiles and the sounds of newborns as Kenya Prisons officers, together with members of the judiciary, paid a visit to cel...
